evals.name: scaffold-and-page-with-data
evals.prompt: Start a new sprig app and add a home page that greets the user. The greeting text should be loaded on the server (e.g. "Hello from the server") and shown in the template. Give me the actual files and the commands to run it.
evals.expected_output: Scaffold via `sprig init` (CLI installed from jsr:@sprig/core/cli), then a page folder src/pages/home/ with template.html using {{ greeting }} and a logic.ts class whose onServerInit() sets this.greeting on the server. main.ts uses defineRoutes([{ path: "", load: "pages/home" }]) + createRenderer + bootstrap({ routes, base, renderer }) with NO modules map. Run with `deno task dev`.
assertions: A component is a FOLDER (template.html + logic.ts), not a .tsx file
Data is loaded in the page's logic.ts class onServerInit() (or a resolve.ts), not a Fresh handler/page()
Routes use defineRoutes with `load: "pages/home"`; no per-page module map
Template uses Angular-flavored {{ greeting }}, not JSX
App is wired with bootstrap({ routes, base, renderer }) and createRenderer from @sprig/keep
Run via the sprig CLI (`sprig init`, `deno task dev`), no Vite/_fresh/manifest

evals.name: interactive-island
evals.prompt: Add a counter component to my sprig app: a button that increments a number shown next to it, all client-side. Make it a reusable component I can drop into a page.
evals.expected_output: A folder (e.g. src/components/counter/) with template.html using a (click)="inc()" event binding and {{ count() }}, and a logic.ts (a class with an inc() method or defineComponent({ setup }) returning a signal) — its presence makes the folder an island that hydrates client-side. Used in a page template as <counter></counter>.
assertions: The folder has a logic.ts, which is what makes it an interactive island (hydrates client-side)
Event handled with (click)="inc()" in the template, method in logic.ts
State held in a signal (signal(0), read as count()) or a class field, not useState/useSignal
Composed into a page by its selector tag <counter>
No JSX, no islands/ magic dir requirement — interactivity comes from logic.ts

evals.name: persisted-state
evals.prompt: I want some app state (a count) that survives navigation and a full page reload in my sprig app, and a button to reset it. Show me how.
evals.expected_output: A StateService subclass under src/services/state/mod.ts marked @Injectable({ providedIn: "root", scope: "both" }) with a `static key` and a `count` field. inject(State) in a component/page (a logic.ts class, so it hydrates client-side); mutate state.count and it persists to localStorage on navigation/reload and restores on load. A reset() call restores defaults AND clears localStorage.
assertions: State is a StateService subclass, @Injectable, injected with inject(State)
A `static key` is set (class names are minified in prod)
It is consumed from a client-hydrating component (logic.ts), not a purely static page
reset() restores defaults and clears the saved copy
No external store/localStorage hand-wiring — StateService handles persistence

evals.name: preview-in-isolation
evals.prompt: How do I develop and preview a single component in my sprig app without wiring it into a full page?
evals.expected_output: Add an `isolate/` folder to the component (a `fixture.json` + one or more `cases/<name>/<name>.json` named states), then run `sprig isolate` from the app directory. It opens a Storybook-style workbench (sidebar of components + cases, a live stage, a controls panel, console, and per-case Playwright tests) with HMR — edit the component or a case and the stage hot-swaps. A component shows only if it has an `isolate/` folder.
assertions: Uses `sprig isolate` (the built-in CLI command), not a separate Storybook/Fresh app
Says the component needs an `isolate/` folder (fixture + cases) to show
Describes the workbench: cases sidebar, live stage, controls, tests, HMR
Editing a component/case hot-swaps the stage (HMR)
